events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose missing lockable disconnect for maintenance on motor circuit in commercial in-service. NEC Art 430 requires disconnecting means within sight from motor and controller, lockable in accordance with 110.25? Actually NEC 430.102(B) requires disconnecting means for motor-driven appliance within sight from motor location and the driven machinery location, or capable of being locked in open position if not within sight. For maintenance, disconnect must be lockable. Missing lockable disconnect is non-compliant and safety hazard: servicing personnel cannot secure energy isolation (LOTO). Decision fail. Risk high (lockout/tagout inability, shock/electrocution, unexpected startup). Work stage in-service. Component conditions: non-compliant, maybe degraded? Since missing component, installed-defective? The categories allowed include installed-defective, non-compliant, worn, degraded, failed. Missing lockable disconnect is non-compliant. Could also installed-defective if disconnect present but not lockable? Scenario says missing lockable disconnect for maintenance. So non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 100 since in-service but defect. Value 0. Sound source irrelevant. Confidence high 0.95. Event time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. Findings: no lockable disconnect within sight/capable of locked open per NEC 430; maintenance lockout cannot be verified; hazard of unexpected energization. Actions: install code-compliant disconnecting means with lockable provision per NEC 430.102/110.25; verify within sight or lockable; coordinate LOTO program; inspect and test before returning to service. Rationale: NEC requires disconnect within sight from motor or lockable; missing lockable disconnect prevents safe LOTO and violates code. References: NEC Art. 430, specifically 430.102, 430.103, 110.25. Need ensure JSON valid.

A missing lockable disconnect prevents safe energy isolation during maintenance, is a code violation, and presents a shock/arc-flash/unexpected-startup hazard.",
